title: Manilkara calcicola
intro: Manilkara calcicola, also known as the calcicole Manilkara, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, classified under the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Manilkara calcicola include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Manilkara calcicola is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Manilkara calcicola is classified within the family Sapotaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as sapotes. The genus Manilkara is a part of this family and is native to tropical regions. Manilkara calcicola is further categorized within the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Additionally, it falls under the order Ebenales and the subclass Dilleniidae. Manilkara calcicola's classification also includes the phylum Tracheophyta.
uses: Manilkara calcicola is known to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and roots are often used in folk remedies to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
